title: cerastium glomeratum var. apetalum
intro: Cerastium glomeratum var. apetalum, also known as sticky mouse-ear, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Cerastium glomeratum var. apetalum is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparation of Cerastium glomeratum var. apetalum typically involves drying the plant material and using it in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions.
